agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module for an HR software system governing how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. The module enforces accrual-based balance management, advance-notice submission rules, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days available to an employee. Cannot be negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which employees earn 1.5 leave days per month of active employment."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to take leave for a specified date range, subject to balance availability, advance notice, and manager approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (within 5 calendar days of the absence) when accompanied by a doctor's note, exempting it from the standard 3-working-day advance notice rule."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-recognized holidays, used to calculate the advance notice period for leave submissions."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's current employment contract terminates. Leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Carryover","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where unused leave days are evaluated; a maximum of 10 days may be carried into the next year, with any excess forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days exceeding the 10-day carryover cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate required to validate a retroactive medical leave request."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a company policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust. It does not represent a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an employee who has worked N complete months, their total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days (before any consumption or forfeiture)."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An employee's leave balance cannot go below zero. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative leave balance would represent consumption of a resource that does not exist — a data integrity violation. The system would be in a corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"At no point in time shall an employee's leave balance be less than zero after any operation (request approval, accrual, forfeiture, cancellation)."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The advance notice period is a configurable business policy, not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before its start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave can be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The retroactive window and documentation requirement are HR policy decisions that could be modified.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request for a past date is accepted if submitted within 5 calendar days of the absence AND a doctor's note is attached; otherwise it is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Managers approve or reject leave requests. Only approved requests consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If a rejected or pending request consumed balance, the system would misrepresent available leave — a state integrity violation.","testable_assertion":"A leave request that is rejected or still pending does not reduce the employee's leave balance. Only transitions to 'approved' status deduct from the balance."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A leave request must not span beyond the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Granting leave beyond a contract's validity represents an impossible real-world state — the employee has no employment relationship on those dates.","testable_assertion":"Any leave request where the end date exceeds the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave exceeding 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The carryover cap is a company policy threshold that could be changed by management decision.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, if an employee's leave balance was N days on December 31, their new balance is min(N, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked for 4 complete months with no leave taken","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient balance error and balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The submission date is fewer than 3 working days before the leave start date","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient notice error"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The submission date is exactly 3 working days before the leave start date and balance is sufficient","then":"The request is accepted and enters pending approval state"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 4 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a valid doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted and enters pending approval state (retroactive submission within the 5-day window)"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 6 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because it exceeds the 5-calendar-day retroactive window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected for missing required documentation"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager rejects a pending leave request","when":"The rejection is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance is unchanged and the request status is 'rejected'"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A manager approves a pending leave request for 3 days","when":"The approval is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced by 3 days and the request status is 'approved'"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 14–16","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 8 days (no forfeiture, under the 10-day cap)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Leave request submitted exactly on the 3-working-day boundary when intervening days include a public holiday","expected_behavior":"The system must correctly compute working days excluding weekends and company holidays. If a holiday falls within the notice window, the effective calendar distance is longer. The request should be accepted if 3 true working days remain."},{"scenario":"Medical leave spanning multiple days where the retroactive window is measured from the last absence day","expected_behavior":"The 5-calendar-day retroactive window should be measured from the last day of the absence period, not the first, to avoid penalizing multi-day illness."},{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request for their exact contract end date","expected_behavior":"The request should be accepted — the rule prohibits spanning *beyond* the contract end date, not *up to* it. A request ending on the contract end date is valid."},{"scenario":"Leave request approved, then contract end date is subsequently moved earlier to before the leave dates","expected_behavior":"The system should re-evaluate or flag previously approved leave that now falls beyond the updated contract end date, reverting balance if cancelled."},{"scenario":"Employee's balance is exactly 0 after year-end forfeiture and they attempt to submit medical leave retroactively","expected_behavior":"The request can be submitted (it meets the retroactive medical leave criteria), but it cannot be approved because there is insufficient balance. The manager approval step should fail with an insufficient balance error unless there is a policy for medical leave to override balance constraints."},{"scenario":"Accrual runs mid-month for an employee who started partway through the month","expected_behavior":"The spec says '1.5 days per month worked.' A partial first month may or may not count — the system should define whether accrual is prorated for partial months or requires a full calendar month."},{"scenario":"Leave request submitted and approved, then subsequently cancelled by the employee","expected_behavior":"The leave balance should be restored by the number of approved days. Cancellation policy (e.g., minimum notice for cancellation) is not specified in the current rules."},{"scenario":"Year-end carryover when an employee has a pending (not yet approved) leave request","expected_behavior":"Pending requests have not consumed balance per BR-5, so forfeiture applies to the full unused balance. If the request is later approved in the new year, it deducts from the carried-over balance."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual calculations or carryover logic could lead to employees being granted more leave than earned, directly impacting labor costs and productivity. Conversely, under-accrual creates compliance risk and employee disputes.","operational":"The advance notice rule (3 working days) enables managers to plan team capacity. Medical leave exceptions ensure employees aren't penalized for genuine illness. The carryover cap incentivizes leave usage within the year, reducing end-of-year leave bottlenecks.","user":"Employees need clear, transparent leave balance visibility and immediate feedback on request validity. Managers need straightforward approve/reject workflows. Poor UX around balance calculations or notice rules will generate HR support tickets.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Negative balance bug allowing leave overdraft, (2) Incorrect working-day calculation leading to wrongful rejections, (3) Year-end forfeiture errors causing legal disputes, (4) Medical leave retroactive window miscalculation. All are testable and preventable with proper invariant enforcement.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct users of the leave system. Accrual accuracy, balance transparency, and fair enforcement of rules directly affect their experience and trust."},{"group":"Line Managers","impact":"Responsible for approving/rejecting leave. Need accurate balance information and timely notifications to make informed decisions."},{"group":"HR Department","impact":"Owns policy configuration (accrual rate, carryover cap, notice periods). Needs audit trails for compliance and dispute resolution."},{"group":"Payroll Team","impact":"Downstream consumer of approved leave data. Incorrect leave records affect salary calculations, especially for unpaid leave scenarios."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Leave liability appears on the balance sheet. Incorrect accrual or carryover calculations affect financial reporting accuracy."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is well-defined with clear, unambiguous rules covering accrual, submission constraints, approval workflow, contract boundaries, and carryover. Confidence is not higher because a few operational details are unspecified (partial-month accrual, leave cancellation, working-day calendar definition) but these are standard implementation details rather than spec deficiencies.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate calculation","Advance notice requirement and medical leave exception","Carryover cap and forfeiture logic","Contract end date boundary constraint","Approval workflow and balance deduction timing"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partial first/last months of employment accrue prorated leave or require full months","How the 5-day retroactive window is measured for multi-day medical absences (from first day or last day)","Whether medical leave can override a zero balance or is still subject to balance constraints","Leave cancellation policy and balance restoration rules","Definition of the working-day calendar (company holidays)"],"assumptions":["The 5-calendar-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured from the last day of the absence","Accrual of 1.5 days occurs once per completed calendar month of employment","Working days exclude weekends (Saturday/Sunday) and company-defined public holidays","Leave balance is tracked in half-day precision (given the 1.5-day accrual unit)","The carryover process runs exactly once at calendar year-end boundary"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The module enforces accrual-based balance management, advance-notice submission rules, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days available to an employee. Cannot be ne

content.thinking: Clear HR leave management domain. No contradictions, no missing context, no ambiguity. Straightforward analysis.

The module enforces accrual-based balance management, advance-notice submission rules, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days available to an employee. Only approved requests consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If a rejected or pending request consumed balance, the system would misrepresent available leave — a state integrity violation.","testable_assertion":"A leave request that is rejected or still pending does not reduce the employee's leave balance. Only transitions to 'approved' status deduct from the balance."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A leave request must not span beyond the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Granting leave beyond a contract's validity represents an impossible real-world state — the employee has no employment relationship on those dates.","testable_assertion":"Any leave request where the end date exceeds the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave exceeding 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The carryover cap is a company policy threshold that could be changed by management decision.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, if an employee's leave balance was N days on December 31, their new balance is min(N, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked for 4 complete months with no leave taken","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient balance error and balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The submission date is fewer than 3 working days before the leave start date","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient notice error"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because it exceeds the 5-calendar-day retroactive window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected for missing required documentation"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager rejects a pending leave If the request is later approved in the new year, it deducts from the carried-over balance."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual calculations or carryover logic could lead to employees being granted more leave than earned, directly impacting labor costs and productivity. Conversely, under-accrual creates compliance risk and employee disputes.","operational":"The advance notice rule (3 working days) enables managers to plan team capacity. The carryover cap incentivizes leave usage within the year, reducing end-of-year leave bottlenecks.","user":"Employees need clear, transparent leave balance visibility and immediate feedback on request validity. Poor UX around balance calculations or notice rules will generate HR support tickets.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Negative balance bug allowing leave overdraft, (2) Incorrect working-day calculation leading to wrongful rejections, (3) Year-end forfeiture errors causing legal disputes, (4) Medical leave retroactive window miscalculation. All are testable and preventable with proper invariant enforcement.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct users of the leave system. Accrual accuracy, balance transparency, and fair enforcement of rules directly affect their experience and trust."},{"group":"Line Managers","impact":"Responsible for approving/rejecting leave. Need accurate balance information and timely notifications to make informed decisions."},{"group":"HR Department","impact":"Owns policy configuration (accrual rate, carryover cap, notice periods). Needs audit trails for compliance and dispute resolution."},{"group":"Payroll Team","impact":"Downstream consumer of approved leave data. Incorrect leave records affect salary calculations, especially for unpaid leave scenarios."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Leave liability appears on the balance sheet. Incorrect accrual or carryover calculations affect financial reporting accuracy."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is well-defined with clear, unambiguous rules covering accrual, submission constraints, approval workflow, contract boundaries, and carryover. Confidence is not higher because a few operational details are unspecified (partial-month accrual, leave cancellation, working-day calendar definition) but these are standard implementation details rather than spec deficiencies.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate calculation","Advance notice requirement and medical leave exception","Carryover cap and forfeiture logic","Contract end date boundary constraint","Approval workflow and balance deduction timing"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partial first/last months of employment accrue prorated leave or require full months","How the 5-day retroactive window is measured for multi-day medical absences (from first day or last day)","Whether medical leave can override a zero balance or is still subject to balance constraints","Leave cancellation policy and balance restoration rules","Definition of the working-day calendar (company holidays)"],"assumptions":["The 5-calendar-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured from the last day of the absence","Accrual of 1.5 days occurs once per completed calendar month of employment","Working days exclude weekends (Saturday/Sunday) and company-defined public holidays","Leave balance is tracked in half-day precision (given the 1.5-day accrual unit)","The carryover process runs exactly once at calendar year-end boundary"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
